@@ -0,0 +1,119 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KQQSXM2PEYR1WAQ7QXW3B8ME
+- 01KQQTXDHP3XBHZ8G40AC4FG4D
+- 01KQQTY2HZBX7M9TW95862JVQ3
+- 01KQQTYKS63RPM62PPVJ7S7J60
+- 01KQQTZ7PSXEQF1WWX14ST8WRT
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: cc80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Spatial-nav follow-up: collapse FocusZone into FocusScope (single primitive with optional children)'
+---
+## Reference
+
+Follow-up to the spatial-nav redesign (design `01KQQSXM2PEYR1WAQ7QXW3B8ME`). The geometric cardinal algorithm and drill / first / last operations have landed; the type-level `FocusZone` / `FocusScope` split was kept during the redesign as a deliberate staging choice. This task collapses them into one primitive.
+
+**Decision:** keep `FocusScope` as the single primitive name. Delete `FocusZone` entirely. `FocusScope` already accepts children (its prop type has `children: ReactNode`). The "is this a container or a leaf?" question becomes "does it have registered children?" â€” answered by querying the registry, not by a type tag.
+
+This is **pure refactor** â€” no behaviour change. The geometric algorithm doesn't distinguish kind; `showFocusBar={false}` is still a per-instance prop; last-focused memory is still keyed by FQM; drill / first / last still work on the focused scope's children.
+
+## What
+
+### Rust kernel side (`swissarmyhammer-focus`)
+
+- `src/scope.rs`:
+  - Delete the `FocusZone` struct.
+  - `RegisteredScope` (the enum or wrapper that distinguishes zones from leaves today) collapses to `FocusScope` only. If it's currently `enum RegisteredScope { Scope(FocusScope), Zone(FocusZone) }`, replace with a struct alias or directly with `FocusScope`.
+  - Drop `RegisteredScope::is_zone()` â€” replace callers with `registry.has_children(fq)` or equivalent.
+  - Whatever per-zone fields don't already exist on `FocusScope` (e.g. `showFocusBar` flag) move onto `FocusScope`.
+
+- `src/registry.rs`:
+  - Collapse `register_scope` and `register_zone` IPC commands into a single `register_scope` (or keep both as transition aliases â€” recommend drop entirely; the React side is updated in this same PR).
+  - Internal storage: one collection, not two. `zones_iter` / `leaves_iter` either go away or get re-implemented as filters over `entries_in_layer` (`with_children` / `without_children`).
+  - Children query: add `fn children_of(&self, parent_fq: &FullyQualifiedMoniker) -> impl Iterator<Item = &FocusScope>` or similar â€” used by drill-in / first / last.
+
+- `src/navigate.rs`:
+  - The leaf-tie-break in `geometric_pick` currently uses `is_zone()` to prefer leaves. Rewrite as "prefer scopes with no registered children" using the new `children_of` query.
+  - Drill-in / first / last already operate on children â€” confirm they don't depend on `is_zone()` after the rename.
+
+- `src/types.rs`:
+  - Sweep any `Direction` / `Pixels` etc. for stale references.
+
+- `tests/*`:
+  - Every `is_zone()` / `RegisteredScope::Zone(...)` callsite needs updating. Fixture builders (`tests/fixtures/mod.rs`) become "register a scope; if it has children later, it acts like a zone."
+  - Tests asserting `spatial_register_zone` IPC name become `spatial_register_scope`.
+
+### TypeScript / React side (`kanban-app/ui`)
+
+- `src/components/focus-zone.tsx`:
+  - Delete the file. Move any unique logic into `focus-scope.tsx`.
+  - All exports (`FocusZone`, `FocusZoneProps`, `FocusZoneContext`, `useParentZoneFq`, etc.) â€” the type re-exports become `FocusScope*`. `FocusZoneContext` becomes `FocusScopeContext` (or stays as the existing `FocusScopeContext` if there's already overlap â€” verify).
+
+- `src/components/focus-scope.tsx`:
+  - Confirm it accepts children (it does â€” see existing `FocusScopeProps.children`).
+  - Add any zone-only props that need to migrate (e.g. zone-specific layout class handling).
+
+- `src/components/use-track-rect-on-ancestor-scroll.ts` and `src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:
+  - Update `spatial_register_zone` IPC adapter to alias / point at `spatial_register_scope`.
+
+- **Sweep all callers** â€” replace `<FocusZone â€¦>` with `<FocusScope â€¦>`, replace `import { FocusZone, FocusZoneProps } from "@/components/focus-zone"` with the equivalent import from `focus-scope`. Known callers (run a grep to confirm complete list):
+  - `nav-bar.tsx` (the outer `<FocusZone moniker="ui:navbar">` and its `<FocusZone moniker="ui:navbar.board-selector">`)
+  - `board-view.tsx` (`BoardSpatialZone` â†’ `<FocusZone moniker="ui:board">`)
+  - `view-container.tsx` (`ViewSpatialZone` â€” note: a separate task `01KQPW61ZNS010FV8PV844KY0K` may delete this entirely; coordinate)
+  - `perspective-container.tsx` (`PerspectiveSpatialZone` â†’ `<FocusZone moniker="ui:perspective">`)
+  - `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` (`PerspectiveBarSpatialZone`)
+  - `left-nav.tsx` (`<FocusZone moniker="ui:left-nav">`)
+  - `column-view.tsx` (column body zones)
+  - `grid-view.tsx` (`GridSpatialZone`)
+  - `entity-card.tsx` (card zones)
+  - `data-table.tsx`
+  - `fields/field.tsx` (`Field` mounts a `<FocusZone>`)
+  - `board-selector.tsx`
+  - `inspectable.tsx`
+  - `inspectors-container.tsx`
+  - Any others â€” verified by `grep -rn "FocusZone" kanban-app/ui/src --include='*.ts*'`
+
+- **Sweep all tests** that assert on `<FocusZone>`, `FocusZoneContext`, `useParentZoneFq`, or `spatial_register_zone` IPC. Many test files (board-view.guards.node.test.ts, perspective-spatial-nav.guards.node.test.ts, etc.) will need updates.
+
+### IPC contract
+
+- After this PR, the IPC surface from React to Rust has only `spatial_register_scope` (no more `spatial_register_zone`). The kernel decides "leaf vs container" by inspecting children.
+- Update `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` (the Tauri command bridge) accordingly.
+
+### Documentation
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/README.md`: rewrite the prose that distinguishes zones from leaves. New framing: "All registered things are `FocusScope`s. A scope with no children behaves as a leaf; a scope with children behaves as a container."
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `grep -r "FocusZone" swissarmyhammer-focus/src kanban-app/ui/src` returns no source-code matches (test fixtures and historical task descriptions excluded).
+- [ ] `grep -r "spatial_register_zone" swissarmyhammer-focus/src kanban-app/ui/src kanban-app/src` returns no source-code matches.
+- [ ] `is_zone()` / `RegisteredScope::Zone` enum variants are gone from the kernel.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` passes.
+- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` passes (full suite â€” this refactor touches many files).
+- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui typecheck` passes.
+- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` passes.
+- [ ] No behaviour change observable in the running app: the four cross-zone bug fixes from the redesign still work; in-card and in-row navigation works; drill / first / last work; focus indicator still paints; `showFocusBar={false}` containers still suppress the visible bar.
+- [ ] README rewritten to reflect single-primitive model.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] No new tests required (pure refactor) â€” but the existing test suite must pass after the sweep. Any test that explicitly asserted "this is a zone, not a leaf" needs to either become "this has children" or be deleted.
+- [ ] Re-run the four cross-zone regression assertions from `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/cross_zone_geometric_nav.rs` and confirm green.
+- [ ] Re-run the drill / first / last assertions and confirm green.
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus && pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test && pnpm -C kanban-app/ui typecheck` and confirm everything green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- This task is large in line count (30+ files) but **not** in conceptual surface â€” it's a mechanical rename + delete + IPC consolidation. One PR is fine because the pieces (kernel struct collapse, React component collapse, IPC unification) are interlocking â€” splitting them creates incompatible intermediate states.
+- Sequence within the PR:
+  1. Kernel side first: collapse `FocusZone` into `FocusScope` in Rust, update IPC command names, run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus`.
+  2. Update IPC adapter in React (`spatial-focus-context.tsx`, `commands.rs`).
+  3. Sweep React callers: rename `<FocusZone>` â†’ `<FocusScope>` and update imports. Use mechanical find/replace; verify each file builds.
+  4. Sweep tests on both sides.
+  5. Run full test suite. Fix any genuine fallout (likely tests that asserted on `is_zone()` or `data-moniker="ui:..."` patterns that no longer differentiate).
+  6. Rewrite README.
+- No `/tdd` for this one â€” it's a refactor with no new behaviour to test. The existing test suite is the safety net.
